# Transcrição Completa: Value Added Questions vs Selfish Questions

 

[00:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "12 Value Added Questions vs Selfish Questions"

**NÚMEROS:** None

**VISUAL:** Título com contraste entre perguntas egoístas vs perguntas de valor

 

[00:02] So earlier today, my first client ever, Lee Hate, he runs a roofing business. It's nationwide. He's got 400 locations. He's one of those crazy guys, like a stormchaser. Hurricane happened on the west coast of Florida just recently. He's over there right now just door knocking.

 

[00:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "Lee Hate - First Client Ever"

**NÚMEROS:** 400 locations nationwide

**VISUAL:** Empresa de telhados, operações em grande escala, hurricane chaser

 

[00:25] Deploying fleets of like hundreds of people into this location at once operationally on the sales side. And he has an education company as well where he teaches other roofers and contractors how to scale their roofing and contracting businesses.

 

[00:40] Long story short, Lee calls me earlier today. He was in the middle of a sales meeting and he wanted me to do some sales training with his guys, which I will happily do.

 

[00:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "The Challenge: Asking Sensitive Questions"

**NÚMEROS:** None

**VISUAL:** Perguntas sobre score de crédito e dinheiro no banco

 

[00:55] So, long story short, Lee essentially says, "Hey, we're trying to find a smooth way to be able to ask people what their credit score is and how much money they have in their bank account."

 

[01:05] Well, these are pretty direct questions and you can imagine that you're either going to come across a lot of hostility or resistance or just dismissal if you as a salesperson asked that very set of questions to your prospects.

 

[01:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "The Problem with Direct Questions"

**NÚMEROS:** None

**VISUAL:** Pessoas mentindo em formulários vs resistência em conversas

 

[01:25] If I as a marketer asked them those questions in a form, there's a high probability people would just lie about it because those are very personal questions. So, we have to find ways where people will be more willing to disclose them.

 

[01:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "Two Categories: Selfish vs Value Added"

**NÚMEROS:** None

**VISUAL:** Comparação lado a lado entre tipos de perguntas

 

[01:45] How do we do that? Well, we're going to categorize this in two ways. Selfish questions versus value added questions. Selfish questions would be, "Hey, how much money do you have in your bank account right now? Hey, I'm wondering what your credit score is. I'm trying to see if you're worthy enough to get approved for funding."

 

[02:05] Selfish questions. There's no real value add for the customer there versus value added ways to ask these questions.

 

[02:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "Pipe.com Example"

**NÚMEROS:** $100,000 loan, 4% interest, $50K minimum bank balance, 650+ credit score

**VISUAL:** Logo Pipe.com, processo de empréstimo com receita recorrente

 

[02:20] So, I gave Lee and his sales team the example of a website called pipe.com. Pipe.com essentially takes recurring revenue, collateralizes it, and they give you a loan. So, long story short, the first time I used Pipe, I had $100,000 that they gave to me as a loan with like 4% interest rates.

 

[02:40] And simply put, it's just like a normal business loan. They just allow me to make payments on it for 3, 6, 9, or 12 months. But the collateral source, kind of like some people take a mortgage and they'll get a heloc against the equity line of credit in their home.

 

[03:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "Pipe's Requirements"

**NÚMEROS:** $50,000 minimum bank balance, 650+ credit score, QuickBooks reconciled

**VISUAL:** Lista de requisitos do Pipe.com

 

[03:05] Pipe.com uses the recurring revenue you have as the collateral. So, PIPE asks you before they're willing to give you any money what your bank balance is. They want at least $50,000 in your accounts before they're even going to consider working with you.

 

[03:20] They also want to make sure that your books are currently reconciled, which means they want you to have a software like QuickBooks Online. They also want to make sure that you have a credit score above 650.

 

[03:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "How Pipe Sells the Information Request"

**NÚMEROS:** $2.7 million annualized revenue example

**VISUAL:** Processo de justificativa antes de pedir informações

 

[03:40] Now to be clear, they don't do that through just a simple form. What they do is they first sell you on why they need the information. They say, "Okay, so you currently have $2.7 million worth of annualized revenue that we'd be willing to give you a loan for."

 

[03:55] "The three things that we need from you in order to see if you qualify for us to work together. We need you to connect your bank account so we can analyze it for the current amounts. We need you to connect QuickBooks online and we also need to run a background check and personal credit history on you."

 

[04:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "Value Added Questions = Reason + Context"

**NÚMEROS:** None

**VISUAL:** Fórmula: Contexto + Razão + Pergunta = Maior compliance

 

[04:20] "That's going to give us a clear determining number that's going to dictate whether we get to work together or not." They give you a reason to give up the information. That's what we call value added questions.

 

[04:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "Lee's Reframed Approach"

**NÚMEROS:** None

**VISUAL:** Script melhorado para perguntas sobre dinheiro e crédito

 

[04:40] So in Lee's example, when he's asking people for their bank balance essentially and for their credit scores, I told him to ask this instead. I said, "Hey, so roofer, we just need to know a few different pieces of information from you to see if it's going to be a good fit or not working together."

 

[04:55] "A lot of what we recommend will cost money to do. We'll tell you to spend money on ads. We'll tell you to buy certain softwares. We have an upfront cost to work with us as a service provider."

 

[05:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "The Better Script"

**NÚMEROS:** None

**VISUAL:** Script completo com justificativas e linguagem sofisticada

 

[05:15] "So before we even consider working with somebody, we like them to be transparent with us and telling us two things. How much cash they currently have in their operating accounts for their business and what their credit scores are so we can see if we can get you approved for funding if you do not have the capital. Is that fair?"

 

[05:35] See, just a simple reframe in the question. Instead of just saying, "Hey, how much money do you have in your bank account? What's your credit score?" You say, "Hey, I want to make sure you understand there's a lot of costs to work with us."

 

[05:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "Key Differences: Sophisticated Language"

**NÚMEROS:** None

**VISUAL:** Comparação: "bank account" vs "operating accounts", "capital" vs "money"

 

[05:55] "We'd like to know how much capital you currently have. Use different words, how much capital you have in your operating accounts for your business. Makes it sound more sophisticated, easier to ask, and also has a reason to ask it."

 

[06:10] "We also like to know what your credit score is so we can get you approved for funding partners. Is that fair?" And we end it with, is that fair? That way, it's an open-ended question.

 

[06:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

**TEXTO:** "Why This Works: Private vs Public"

**NÚMEROS:** None

**VISUAL:** Conversa one-on-one vs formulários online, honestidade vs mentiras

 

[06:30] Value added ways to ask. Make sure you tie a reason into why you're asking some of these harder hitting questions because getting answers to those questions is important. It helps you as the salesperson close deals.

 

[06:45] But you got to have sophisticated ways to ask so you can actually get the answers. If I, as the marketer, do it, people typically lie on the internet. If you as the salesperson do it in a one-on-one conversation where it's private, there's a much higher probability that people will actually answer you honestly. See you in the next video.

 

**🔥 ANÁLISE FURION POWER: Jeremy DESTRÓI a ingenuidade de vendedores que fazem perguntas como amadores! A diferença entre perguntas egoístas e de valor agregado é BRUTAL - enquanto medíocres pedem informações como mendigos, MESTRES como Jeremy constroem contexto que faz prospects QUEREREM compartilhar dados sensíveis. O exemplo do Pipe.com é GENIAL - mostra como empresas bilionárias vendem a necessidade da informação antes de pedi-la. Jeremy transforma resistência em colaboração!**
